steps.title: Identify the independent clauses and their connector
steps.content: The blank separates two independent clauses connected by the word “however.”

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule for 'however'
steps.content: When 'however' connects clauses, place a semicolon before it and a comma after it (IC; however, IC). Choice B follows this rule. The other choices violate it by either reversing, omitting, or failing to include the necessary punctuation. That matches choice B — B is correct.
